name: Keratoconjunctivitis Sicca (Dry Eye)
description: KCS, commonly called dry eye, is inadequate tear production causing corneal dryness, inflammation, ulceration, and scarring. It is often immune-mediated in dogs and can lead to permanent vision impairment if poorly controlled.
treatment_overview: Lifelong twice-daily cyclosporine or tacrolimus eye drops stimulate tear production in immune-mediated cases. Artificial tears and antibiotics manage secondary infections. Parotid duct transposition is a surgical salvage option.
prevention_tips: Avoid sulphonamide antibiotics which can cause KCS
